channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: You don’t need an SSN to open a credit card: Scammers know that
item.description: More credit card issuers now approve applicants without a Social Security number, giving scammers new ways to commit identity theft using stolen data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese robot breaks human world record in Beijing half-marathon
item.description: China's push to dominate robotics accelerates as a humanoid robot beats the human half-marathon world record, sparking awe and unease among spectators.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chernobyl's radioactive landscape is a testament to nature’s resilience and survival spirit
item.description: Wildlife is thriving again four decades after the nuclear disaster at Ukraine’s Chernobyl power plant in what became the exclusion zone created by the forced mass evacuations of the population.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos And Evgeniy Maloletka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mexico’s climate supercomputer could change forecasting
item.description: Mexico is building the Coatlicue supercomputer to deliver sharper weather forecasts and earlier storm warnings and support for artificial intelligence research.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to hide your sensitive info (for real) when using ChatGPT and other AI chatbots
item.description: Like many, I’ve never met a chatbot I trust completely. Not only do they have a propensity to hallucinate by making up facts, but you can never be sure what their parent companies do with the information you provide.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: What San Francisco’s AI billboards say about the state of the industry
item.description: Here in San Francisco, we live in a bubble, and we know it. While much of the rest of the country sees the city through the lens of Fox News cameramen searching out homeless encampments, we actually live in a very beautiful, very wealthy, and, currently, very AI-obsessed place.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday release for April is a whopper
item.description: Windows admins are going to be busy this month, dealing with the largest Patch Tuesday cycle we can recall. The April release involves 165 updates and roughly 340 unique CVEs from Microsoft — including two zero-days, one of which is already being actively exploited in the wild.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: White House chief of staff meets with Anthropic CEO over its new AI technology
item.description: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles has met with Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to discuss the company's new AI model, Mythos.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What I learned by vibe-coding my own word processor
item.description: Hello again, and welcome back to Fast Company’s Plugged In. Before we go any further, an invitation: On Thursday, April 23, at 1 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Data centers are costing local governments billions
item.description: Tax benefits for hyperscalers and other data center operators are costing local administrations billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Robot Zuckerberg shows how IT can free up CEOs’ time
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg, the CEO of Meta, is building an AI version of himself. The virtual CEO is being trained on Zuckerberg’s mannerisms and will be loaded with his views on corporate strategy, the Financial Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: They bought property in the metaverse. Then it collapsed
item.description: Five years ago, tech angel investor Chris Adamo and a few friends jumped on a burgeoning trend in the digital asset world: they used a virtual real estate broker to buy 23 parcels of property in a metaverse called The Sandbox.
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Equinix offering targets automated AI-centric network operations
item.description: Equinix has introduced Fabric Intelligence, a service designed to automate and optimize network infrastructure as enterprises scale artificial intelligence workloads across multiple distributed locations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech company cuts 1,000 jobs in AI-driven restructuring
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter gives readers the latest AI technology advancements, covering the challenges and opportunities AI presents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This new Google Pixel phone is exclusive to Japan
item.description: Despite Google’s status as one of the true giants of U.S. tech, it’s never quite been able to make its Pixel phones a mainstream success. Last year, for instance, the company enjoyed record U.S. sales in September after the launch of the Pixel 10 line, according to Counterpoint Research.
item.dc:creator: Sam Byford, Fast Company

item.title: Younger adult colon cancer deaths are concentrated in people with less education, study says
item.description: A new study finds the worrisome rise in colorectal cancer deaths among younger adults is concentrated in people with less education.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft Teams cheat sheet: How to get started
item.description: If your organization uses Microsoft 365 (a.k.a. Office), chances are you’ve encountered Teams, at least for video meetings. But it’s capable of a lot more, providing an effective way for groups of people to collaborate on work and advance business objectives.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google Cloud introduces QueryData to help AI agents create reliable database queries
item.description: A new tool from Google Cloud aims to improve the accuracy of AI agents querying databases in multi-agent systems or applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World
